extra.message: User input `name` from POST form data is concatenated into SQL via Python string formatting (`%` operator) instead of parameterized query. The query string is formatted BEFORE being passed to cursor.execute(), allowing complete SQL injection. The `name` value originates from `views.py:57` (`data['name']`) and is unsanitized.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: Jinja2 autoescaping is explicitly disabled (`autoescape=False`). Combined with templates that omit the `| e` filter on user-controlled data, this enables stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS). Multiple template locations render user input without escaping (see related findings).

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Stored XSS: `course.description` is rendered without escaping (`{{ course.description }}`). The description value comes from user POST input in `views.py:89-90` and is stored in the database. With `autoescape=False`, any HTML or JavaScript in the description will execute in the browser.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Stored XSS: `review.review_text` is rendered without escaping (`{{ review.review_text }}`). The review text comes from user POST input in `views.py:121-129` and is stored in the database. With `autoescape=False`, malicious JavaScript in a review will execute in any browser viewing the course page.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Stored XSS: `course.title` is rendered without escaping (`{{ course.title }}`). The title comes from user POST input in `views.py:89` and is stored in the database. With `autoescape=False`, injected HTML/JavaScript in a course title will execute in the browser.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Stored XSS: Student `name` is rendered without escaping (`{{ name }}`). The name comes from user POST input in `views.py:57` and is stored in the database. With `autoescape=False`, malicious JavaScript in a student name will execute in any browser viewing the students list.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Stored XSS: `course.title` is rendered without escaping (`{{ course.title }}`) on the student detail page. The title comes from user POST input in `views.py:89` and is stored in the database.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Sensitive information disclosure: `{{ error.__dict__ }}` dumps the full internal state of Python exception objects to the user in 5xx error pages. This exposes stack traces, internal variable values, request details, database query fragments, configuration paths, and other implementation details that aid attackers in crafting further exploits.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: Password hashing uses MD5 (`hashlib.md5`), which is cryptographically broken and vulnerable to rainbow table and collision attacks. Any leaked password hashes can be reversed instantly with modern hardware or online lookup tables.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
CWE-916: Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort

extra.message: CSRF middleware is commented out (`# csrf_middleware`). While the `csrf_token()` function generates tokens and forms include them in hidden fields, the server-side middleware that validates tokens on POST requests is never registered. All POST endpoints are unprotected against Cross-Site Request Forgery, allowing attackers to forge authenticated requests on behalf of victims.

Session cookie is created with `httponly=False`, making the session ID accessible to client-side JavaScript via `document.cookie`. Combined with any XSS vulnerability in the application, this allows attackers to steal session cookies and hijack authenticated user sessions. No `secure` or `samesite` flags are set either.

cwe: CWE-614: Sensitive Cookie in HTTPS Session Without 'Secure' Attribute

extra.message: Missing authentication/authorization on course creation. The `courses` view allows any unauthenticated user to create courses via POST. The template conditionally shows the form only to admin users (`auth_user.is_admin`), but no server-side check exists. An attacker can POST directly to `/courses/` to create arbitrary courses without any authentication.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Missing authentication on student creation. The `students` view allows any unauthenticated user to create new students via POST. While the template hides the form from non-authenticated users, there is no server-side authentication check. An attacker can POST directly to `/students/` to create arbitrary student records.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Missing authentication on review creation. The `review` view has no `@authorize()` decorator. Any unauthenticated user can POST to `/courses/{course_id}/review` to submit course reviews. There is no server-side check for authentication or authorization.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Missing authentication on student evaluation. The `evaluate` view has no `@authorize()` decorator. The template only shows the evaluation form to admin users, but any unauthenticated user can POST directly to `/students/{id}/evaluate/{course_id}` to submit grades. There is no server-side authentication or authorization check.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
